title: Intro to Programming
description: Foundations of problem solving and programming in a high-level language: logic, control flow, functions, and basic data structures. Aligned with your textbook and lecture materials.

chapters.title: Introduction to computing & your environment
topics: What is a program? Compilers vs interpreters
IDE / editor, terminal, and running code
Syntax, semantics, and reading error messages
Style, naming, and commenting for beginners

chapters.title: Variables, types, and expressions
topics: Primitive types (int, float, bool, char/string basics)

chapters.title: Boolean logic & selection (if / else)
topics: Common bugs: = vs ==, off-by-one, boundary cases

chapters.title: Repetition: loops
topics: Break, continue, and when to avoid them

chapters.title: Functions / methods & decomposition
topics: Unit testing mindset (even without a framework)

chapters.title: Files and exceptions (basics)
topics: Paths, working directory, and file not found
Try / catch or equivalent error handling

chapters.title: Structs, classes, or records (intro OOP)
topics: Grouping data: structs / simple classes / dictionaries (contextual)

chapters.title: Problem solving, debugging & complexity intro
topics: Preparing for the final project / exam
